diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,50 @@
+name: Claude Code
+
+on:
+  issue_comment:
+    types: [created]
+  pull_request_review_comment:
+    types: [created]
+  issues:
+    types: [opened, assigned]
+  pull_request_review:
+    types: [submitted]
+
+jobs:
+  claude:
+    if: |
+      (github.event_name == 'issue_comment' && contains(github.event.comment.body, '@claude')) ||
+      (github.event_name == 'pull_request_review_comment' && contains(github.event.comment.body, '@claude')) ||
+      (github.event_name == 'pull_request_review' && contains(github.event.review.body, '@claude')) ||
+      (github.event_name == 'issues' && (contains(github.event.issue.body, '@claude') || contains(github.event.issue.title, '@claude')))
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    permissions:
+      contents: read
+      pull-requests: read
+      issues: read
body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Grant write scopes required for @claude responses**

This workflow only grants `read` on `contents`, `pull-requests`, and `issues`, but the Claude action is expected to post tracking/reply comments and potentially open/update PR artifacts when invoked via `@claude`. In that scenario, GitHub API calls that write to issues/PRs will be rejected (`Resource not accessible by integration`), so the assistant can be triggered but cannot complete its core interaction loop.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,44 @@
+name: Claude Code Review
+
+on:
+  pull_request:
+    types: [opened, synchronize, ready_for_review, reopened]
+    # Optional: Only run on specific file changes
+    # paths:
+    #   - "src/**/*.ts"
+    #   - "src/**/*.tsx"
+    #   - "src/**/*.js"
+    #   - "src/**/*.jsx"
+
+jobs:
+  claude-review:
+    # Optional: Filter by PR author
+    # if: |
+    #   github.event.pull_request.user.login == 'external-contributor' ||
+    #   github.event.pull_request.user.login == 'new-developer' ||
+    #   github.event.pull_request.author_association == 'FIRST_TIME_CONTRIBUTOR'
+
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    permissions:
+      contents: read
+      pull-requests: read
+      issues: read
body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Permit PR/issue writes for automated code review output**

The review job is configured with read-only PR/issue permissions, but automated review mode needs to publish findings back to the pull request (inline comments/summary or tracking comment). With `pull-requests: read` and `issues: read`, the review can run but cannot post results to the PR, which breaks the purpose of this workflow.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.
